validate.description: Run the repository validation pipeline used before merge to main.
outputs: Repository validation result across syntax, YAML, Ansible lint, shell, JSON, canonical data-model, and generated-document checks.

install-hooks.description: Install the repo-managed pre-commit and pre-push hook layers for this checkout.
outputs: Git hooks installed for the current checkout, including the fast local pre-commit set and the remote-first pre-push gate.

pre-push-gate.description: Run the ADR 0087 repository validation gate through the build server with local Docker fallback.
outputs: A blocking pass or fail result across the nine configured pre-push validation checks, together with a recorded last-run status payload.

gate-status.description: Show the configured validation gate checks plus the last local, bypass, and post-merge results.
outputs: A human-readable summary of enabled validation checks, the last local gate run, the last post-merge gate run, and the latest explicit bypass receipt.

post-merge-gate.description: Re-run the ADR 0087 validation manifest after merge to main from the Windmill worker checkout.
outputs: A post-merge pass or fail result for the same validation manifest used by the interactive pre-push gate.

nightly-integration-tests.description: Run the ADR 0111 nightly integration suite, post the summary to Mattermost, and emit GlitchTip failures when checks regress.
outputs: A JSON report under .local/integration-tests/nightly-last-run.json describing passed, failed, and skipped ADR 0111 checks.
An optional Mattermost summary and GlitchTip failure event when the worker has the corresponding webhook secrets or environment overrides.

fault-injection.description: Run the ADR 0171 controlled fault-injection subset on docker-runtime-lv3, record a JSON report, and optionally publish failure notifications.
outputs: A JSON report under .local/fault-injection/latest.json describing the selected ADR 0171 scenario runs and their recovery outcome.
A local ledger-style event stream under .local/state/ledger/fault-injection.events.jsonl, plus optional Mattermost and GlitchTip notifications when webhook secrets are available.

run-triage.description: Run the ADR 0114 rule-based incident triage engine for one alert payload, write the report, and optionally post the summary to Mattermost.
outputs: A JSON triage report under .local/triage/reports/, together with an optional Mattermost summary and mutation-audit event.

runbook-executor.description: Execute one structured runbook definition from YAML, JSON, or Markdown front matter, persist the step trace, and support resume after escalation.
outputs: A persisted run record under .local/runbooks/runs/<run_id>.json together with mutation-audit events for start, resume, completion, and failed or escalated steps.
verification_commands: uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest tests/test_runbook_executor.py tests/test_runbook_executor_windmill.py tests/test_lv3_cli.py -q
uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/lv3_cli.py runbook execute docs/runbooks/renew-certificate.yaml --args service=grafana --dry-run

triage-calibration.description: Calibrate ADR 0114 rule precision and recall against resolved incident cases and publish the weekly summary.
outputs: A calibration summary under .local/triage/calibration/latest.json with per-rule precision and recall or an explicit insufficient-data status.

generate-status-docs.description: Regenerate the README status fragments that are derived from canonical repository state.
outputs: Generated README platform status, control-plane lane summary, version summary, document index, and merged-workstream sections refreshed from canonical repository state.

install-proxmox.description: Install Proxmox VE on the Debian host from the repo-managed package path.
outputs: Proxmox package repositories and core platform packages converged on the host.

configure-network.description: Converge the host bridge and NAT network model for guest connectivity.
outputs: vmbr0, vmbr10, IPv4 forwarding, and guest NAT policy converged on the host.

converge-guest-network-policy.description: Converge the default-deny guest network policy on the Proxmox host and all managed guests.
outputs: Per-VM Proxmox firewall rules, NIC firewall enablement, and guest nftables policies converged from the canonical network policy matrix.

configure-ingress.description: Converge public ingress forwarding from the host to the NGINX edge VM.
outputs: TCP 80 and 443 forwarding from the public host IP to nginx-lv3.

configure-edge-publication.description: Publish repo-managed public hostnames at the NGINX edge.
outputs: NGINX virtual hosts, proxy routes, and edge certificate domains updated on nginx-lv3.

configure-tailscale.description: Converge host Tailscale installation and subnet-router settings.
outputs: Tailscale package, auth state helpers, and advertised guest subnet routing on the host.

converge-headscale.description: Deploy the repo-managed Headscale control plane on the Proxmox host, publish it through the shared NGINX edge, and reconcile the public DNS record for headscale.lv3.org.
outputs: Headscale control plane on proxmox_florin, public DNS publication for headscale.lv3.org, shared-edge proxy routing, and repo-managed ACL policy for the management mesh.

provision-guests.description: Provision the Debian cloud template and declared guest VMs on Proxmox.
outputs: Template VM 9000 and declared guest instances created or updated on the Proxmox host.

harden-access.description: Converge the host access baseline for routine non-root operations.
outputs: Host-side SSH, sudo, and Proxmox access baseline enforced for routine operations.

harden-guest-access.description: Converge the Linux guest access baseline for the managed VM fleet.
outputs: Guest SSH and sudo baseline enforced across the managed Linux VMs.

harden-security.description: Converge the Proxmox host security baseline including firewall, notifications, ACME, and TOTP.
outputs: Firewall, ACME certificate, notifications, and TOTP state converged on the Proxmox host.

provision-api-access.description: Create and verify the durable Proxmox API automation identity and token.
outputs: Durable Proxmox API automation user and locally mirrored token payload.

converge-monitoring.description: Converge the monitoring VM, guest telemetry paths, Grafana dashboards, Prometheus service-graph metrics, Tempo tracing, and centralized Loki log shipping.
outputs: Monitoring VM services converged, dashboards imported, guest telemetry wired into InfluxDB, Tempo tracing plus Prometheus service-graph metrics exposed through Grafana, and centralized Loki log shipping active for the host and managed guests.

converge-postgres-vm.description: Converge the dedicated PostgreSQL guest baseline on postgres-lv3.

continuous-drift-detection.description: Run the ADR 0091 multi-source drift suite through the build server and publish structured drift events plus a stored receipt.

restore-verification.description: Run the ADR 0099 backup-restore verification workflow, write one receipt, emit the mutation-audit event, and optionally publish NATS and Mattermost summaries.
outputs: A dated restore-verification receipt recording the selected backup, restore duration, boot time, per-target smoke tests, synthetic replay success rate, latency distribution, and overall pass or fail status.

security-posture-scan.description: Run the ADR 0102 Lynis and Trivy security posture workflow, store a receipt, and publish security events.
outputs: A timestamped security posture receipt covering Lynis hardening scores, new host findings, and HIGH or CRITICAL runtime image CVEs, together with optional NATS, Mattermost, GlitchTip, and Influx outputs.

weekly-security-scan.description: Run the ADR 0142 public-surface security scan, store a receipt, and publish high or critical findings.

audit-token-inventory.description: Run the ADR 0141 token inventory audit against the canonical token policy and emit a receipt plus ledger event.

A mutation-ledger file event describing the audit summary.

token-exposure-response.description: Run the ADR 0141 exposure response workflow for one governed token, including revocation, replacement, incident receipt generation, and session invalidation when required.
outputs: An exposure incident receipt under receipts/security-incidents/ capturing the exposure window, revocation, replacement, session invalidation, and impact steps.
Mutation-ledger file events for revocation and incident creation when the required hooks succeed.

rotate-keycloak-client-secret.description: Rotate one ADR 0141 governed Keycloak client secret through the configured token hook contract.

rotate-windmill-token.description: Rotate one ADR 0141 governed Windmill API token through the configured token hook contract.

rotate-grafana-service-token.description: Rotate one ADR 0141 governed Grafana service account token through the configured token hook contract.

rotate-platform-cli-token.description: Rotate one ADR 0141 governed local platform CLI token through the configured token hook contract.
outputs: A hook-backed or planned local platform CLI token rotation result for the requested token id.

rotate-secret.description: Plan or apply one repo-managed secret rotation, update the owning service without a full reconverge, and persist the rotated value in OpenBao.

disaster-recovery-runbook.description: Render the ADR 0100 ordered recovery plan, current DR evidence summary, and table-top review state.

configure-backups.description: Retained legacy external CIFS backup workflow from ADR 0020.

database-dns.description: Publish the private database.lv3.org DNS record toward the Patroni-managed PostgreSQL VIP.

capacity-report.description: Render the repository-managed capacity model report, combining committed allocations, planned growth, and optional live metrics from the monitoring VM.
outputs: A text, markdown, json, or Prometheus-format summary of host capacity targets, active commitments, planned guests, and reserved ephemeral headroom.

weekly-capacity-report.description: Generate the weekly Windmill payload used to post the current platform capacity summary to Mattermost.
outputs: A JSON payload containing the destination channel, metrics source, and markdown-formatted weekly capacity report.

deploy-and-promote.description: Validate a staged service change, enforce the ADR 0073 promotion gate, and then apply the approved production promotion.
outputs: A validated promotion gate decision, a production live-apply receipt, and a promotion receipt linking staging evidence to the production mutation.

live-apply-waves.description: Execute one reviewed dependency-wave manifest so each wave acquires its locks up front and runs its playbooks in parallel.
outputs: A JSON execution summary containing the resolved wave order, the per-playbook make command, lock resources, and success or failure for every wave item.
verification_commands: uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_dependency_wave_apply.py -q

collection-publish.description: Build the lv3.platform Ansible collection, validate the local tarball path, and publish it to the configured internal Galaxy server.
outputs: A versioned lv3.platform collection tarball under build/collections and, when a token is present, a publish request against the internal Galaxy server.

packer-template-rebuild.description: Validate and rebuild the repo-managed Proxmox VM template catalog in dependency order, then refresh the template manifest metadata.
outputs: Rebuilt Proxmox VM templates in dependency order and refreshed metadata in config/vm-template-manifest.json.

fixture-expiry-reaper.description: Destroy any ADR 0088 ephemeral fixture whose lifetime window has expired and record one reaper-run receipt for the scan.
outputs: Expired fixture receipts are removed from receipts/fixtures and archived under .local/fixtures/archive/.
A reaper-run receipt is written under .local/fixtures/reaper-runs/reaper-run-<timestamp>.json with the expired and skipped fixture ids.

ephemeral-vm-reaper.description: Sweep the governed ADR 0106 ephemeral VM pool, retag unowned temporary VMs with a one-hour grace period, and destroy expired VMs.
outputs: Expired ephemeral VMs are destroyed even if the originating workflow never reaches explicit teardown.
Untagged VMs in the ephemeral VMID pool are retagged with a one-hour grace period and included in the reaper summary receipt.

check-platform-drift.description: Run the ADR 0071 observation loop checks and emit structured findings from the controller command lane.
outputs: Structured drift findings under .local/platform-observation/latest plus an Open WebUI review digest under .local/open-webui/platform-findings-daily.md.

open-maintenance-window.description: Open one ADR 0080 maintenance window in the private NATS KV bucket before a planned live change.

preferred_entrypoint.command: make open-maintenance-window SERVICE=grafana REASON='planned restart' DURATION_MINUTES=30

outputs: One active maintenance window stored in the private maintenance-windows KV bucket together with a platform.maintenance.opened event on the internal event bus.
verification_commands: uv run --with pyyaml --with nats-py python scripts/maintenance_window_tool.py list

close-maintenance-window.description: Close one ADR 0080 maintenance window and emit the matching platform.maintenance.closed or platform.maintenance.force_closed event.
outputs: The targeted maintenance window is removed from the private maintenance-windows KV bucket and a close event is emitted on the internal event bus.
verification_commands: uv run --with pyyaml --with nats-py python scripts/maintenance_window_tool.py list

operator-onboard.description: Apply one ADR 0108 human-operator onboarding change across the repo-authoritative roster, Keycloak, OpenBao, step-ca, Tailscale, and Mattermost.

preferred_entrypoint.command: make operator-onboard NAME='Alice Example' EMAIL='alice@example.com' ROLE=operator SSH_KEY=@/tmp/alice.pub

outputs: A normalized active operator entry committed in config/operators.yaml together with controller-local ADR 0108 state under .local/state/operator-access/.
The Keycloak user, OpenBao entity, and repo-managed operator role definitions are converged for the requested operator.

operator-offboard.description: Disable one ADR 0108 human operator across Keycloak, OpenBao, Tailscale, and the repo-authoritative roster while preserving audit history.
outputs: The targeted operator is marked inactive in config/operators.yaml and mirrored in controller-local ADR 0108 state.
Keycloak is disabled and the OpenBao entity is driven to the inactive state for the targeted operator.

sync-operators.description: Reconcile the merged ADR 0108 operator roster into the live identity systems from the authoritative config/operators.yaml file.
outputs: Every active or inactive roster entry is applied idempotently to the external identity systems from the current mainline operator roster.

quarterly-access-review.description: Build the ADR 0108 quarterly access review summary and optionally post it to Mattermost.
outputs: A review summary enumerating active operators, stale activity, and the currently flagged access-review items.

scheduler-watchdog-loop.description: Run one ADR 0172 watchdog pass over running mutation jobs, abort stale executions, and emit a heartbeat.
outputs: A JSON summary of running mutation jobs, including stale-job aborts, budget violations, warnings, and the latest watchdog heartbeat file.

platform-observation-loop.description: Run the ADR 0126 observation-to-action closure loop over one batch of observation findings and persist per-service loop runs.
outputs: A JSON summary of processed observation findings plus durable loop run records under .local/state/closure-loop/.

converge-semaphore.description: Converge the private Semaphore runtime on docker-runtime-lv3, provision its PostgreSQL backend on postgres-lv3, publish the Tailscale proxy on the Proxmox host, and seed the self-test project plus controller-local auth artifacts.
outputs: Private Semaphore runtime on docker-runtime-lv3 with a PostgreSQL backend on postgres-lv3, a Tailscale-published controller URL on the Proxmox host, controller-local auth artifacts under .local/semaphore, and a seeded self-test project.

semaphore-manage.description: Run governed Semaphore API actions such as listing projects, listing templates, launching the seeded self-test job, or reading one task output.
outputs: Governed Semaphore project inspection, seeded template execution, and task-output retrieval through the repo-managed wrapper instead of ad hoc API calls.
